author: y4rr
content:
Done. JSON-Schema constrained-decoding logits processor landed in ferrotorch-llama::grammar.

Modules:
- ferrotorch-llama/src/grammar/schema.rs - JSON-Schema parser into a typed Schema enum (subset: object, array, string, number, integer, boolean, null, string enum, nullable via type-union).
- ferrotorch-llama/src/grammar/state.rs - JsonGrammar character-level state machine over a partial JSON value. Tracks frame stack (object/array nesting), per-frame phase, and required-key satisfaction. Handles JSON edge cases the property test surfaced: forbids leading zeros (-007 invalid), forbids trailing decimal (1. invalid).
- ferrotorch-llama/src/grammar/json_schema.rs - public JsonSchemaProcessor that wraps a tokenizer vocabulary and produces a per-step TokenMask (Vec<u32>) by simulating each token chars against the grammar.

GPU integration:
- ferrotorch-cubecl::quant::kernel_apply_token_mask + apply_token_mask_to_gpu launcher: portable CubeCL kernel that maps Array<F> + Array<u32> mask to Array<F> with disallowed positions set to F::min_value() (effectively -inf for sampling). Verified end-to-end on the actual RTX 3090 via token_mask_kernel_runs_on_gpu_and_replaces_disallowed_with_min_value.

Verification:
- 36 grammar tests pass: per-state semantics, schema round-trips, key/value rejection, nested objects, arrays, nullable, string enums, leading zeros forbidden, trailing decimal forbidden.
- Property test: 5 schemas (ExtractionResponse-shaped, nested object, integer array, closed enum, nullable string) x 1000 random sampled completions = 5000 trials. Every accumulated string either completes as valid JSON or trails off at a valid prefix (no invalid JSON ever emitted).
- Step-by-step deterministic tests verify exact valid completions and rejection of unknown keys.
- 7 GPU runtime tests including the mask-apply kernel verified on RTX 3090.

Limitations explicit in module docs (each captured by a test asserting rejection):
- No whitespace (compact JSON only).
- Numbers: no exponent notation (1e5).
- Strings: no escapes and no embedded quotes in strings.
- No oneOf, anyOf, allOf, $ref, regex pattern, format constraints, minLength etc.

These are mechanical extensions on the existing state-machine pattern, tracked but out of scope for this turn.

Backend evidence per the rust-gpu-discipline skill:
- kernel_apply_token_mask uses launch_unchecked::<f32, CudaRuntime> - real cudarc dispatch.
- Grammar state machine is CPU (justified: branchy logical work, no tensor data).
- Mask application is GPU (justified: tensor op over vocab logits).
- Test reachability: token_mask_kernel_runs_on_gpu_and_replaces_disallowed_with_min_value constructs CudaRuntime client, runs kernel, reads back, asserts byte-for-byte match.

cargo test -p ferrotorch-llama --lib: 59 passed (was 23, added 36 grammar). cargo clippy -p ferrotorch-llama --lib: 0 warnings.
